---
# Document details
title: "Patagonian toothfish size at first maturity inferred from individual growth increments"
# date: "`r format(Sys.time(), "%A %d %B %Y")`"
author: Jessica E. Marsh$^{1,2,*}$, Stephen D. Gregory$^{1,2}$, Marta Soeffker$^{1,3}$
authors: 
  - name: Jessica E. Marsh$^{1,2,*}$
    orcid: 0000-0003-1154-4444
  - name: Stephen D. Gregory$^{1,2}$
    orcid: 0000-0002-8230-0191
  - name: Marta Soeffker$^{1,3}$
    orcid: 0000-0002-7131-5486
email: "[jessica.marsh@cefas.gov.uk](mailto:jessica.marsh@cefas.gov.uk)"
abstract: |

  $^{1}$ The Centre for Environment, Fisheries and Aquaculture Science, Barrack Road, Weymouth, Dorset DT4 8UB, UK

  $^{2}$ Department of Life and Environmental Sciences, Faculty of Science and Technology, Bournemouth University, Poole BH12 5BB, UK

  $^{3}$ The Centre for Environment, Fisheries and Aquaculture Science, Pakefield Road, Lowestoft, Suffolk NR33 0HT, UK

  \* Corresponding author: tel: +44 1929 ... ...; email: {{< meta email >}}
abstract-title: Affiliations
  
# Output details
format: 
  # docx:
  #   highlight-style: github
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    self-contained: true

# Quarto execute options
execute: 
  eval: true
  echo: false
  message: false
  warning: false
  code-fold: true
  code-summary: "expand for full code"
  fig-align: "center"

# other quarto options
from: markdown+emoji

# References
csl: ecology.csl # more at www.zotero.org/styles
bibliography: references.bib
link-citations: true

# Analysis details
version: "`r version$version.string`"
packages: ""
dependencies: ""
---

```{r doc_setup}

# document setup ----------------------------------------------------------

# clear workspace
rm(list = ls())

# document setup
library(here)
library(readxl)
library(data.table)

# inline tables and figures
inline <- TRUE
endofms <- ifelse(inline, FALSE, TRUE)

```

**Short title**

**Keywords**

## Abstract

To estimate size at first maturity of toothfish using a segmented regression between size at release and growth rate, and quantify whether the breakpoint in the relationship is influenced by sex or temperature. 


<!-- Analysis starts here Analysis starts here Analysis starts here Analysis starts here -->

```{r analysis}

# choose analysis ---------------------------------------------------------

# choose analyses
do__orgdata <- 0
do__explplots <- 0
do__simtest <- 0
do__modelfit <- 0
do__modelchk <- 0
do__modelinf <- 0

# r setup -----------------------------------------------------------------
source(here("scripts", "r-setup.R"))

# libraries & functions ---------------------------------------------------
source(here("scripts", "libraries-and-functions.R"))

# analysis options --------------------------------------------------------
source(here("scripts", "analysis-options.R"))

# analysis options --------------------------------------------------------
source(here("scripts", "global-variables.R"))

# if any analyses chosen, then do analysis
anly_choices <- sapply(ls(pattern = "do__"), function(v) eval(parse(text = v)))
do__anly <- ifelse(any(as.logical(anly_choices)), 1, 0)
if (do__anly) {
  
  # organise data -----------------------------------------------------------
  if (do__orgdata) source(here("scripts", "00-orgdata.R"))
  
  # explplots script --------------------------------------------------------
  if (do__explplots) source(here("scripts", "01-explplots.R"))

  # simtests script ---------------------------------------------------------
  if (do__simtest) source(here("scripts", "02-simtest.R"))

  # modelfit script ---------------------------------------------------------
  if (do__modelfit) source(here("scripts", "03-modelfit.R"))
  
  # modelchk script ---------------------------------------------------------
  if (do__modelchk) source(here("scripts", "04-modelchk.R"))

  # modelinf script ---------------------------------------------------------
  if (do__modelinf) source(here("scripts", "05-modelinf.R"))

}

# close ---------------------------------------------------------------------
source(here("scripts", "close.R"))

```

\newpage

<!-- Document starts here Document starts here Document starts here Document starts here -->

# Introduction (600)


# Methods (900)

## Data

-	Summary of toothfish data collection and datasets 
-	Subset data to include only fish released during 2004 to 2018 and recaptured within five years? Do we exclude fish recaptured within 180 days to account for ‘tag shock’ effects on growth (Collins2010?), or include these as all fish are expected to respond in the same way to tagging? 
-	Temperature variable - degree days between release and recapture. Copernicus daily SST from 1981 to present at https://cds.climate.copernicus.eu/cdsapp#!/dataset/satellite-sea-surface-temperature?tab=overview 
-	Table: summary of macro gonad staging in CCAMLR, compare with results from Pete’s paper 

## Growth model

To identify a potential change point in the relationship between release length and growth rate (hereafter *growth*) that could indicate a shift from somatic to reproductive growth, we estimated growth in a segmented regression by extending the approach outlined in @Scott2012. Specifically, we conditioned growth on a set of explanatory variables thought to describe it, including sex and temperature (**Table ?**), while simultaneously including fish length to identify possibly year- and sex-specific change points in its relationship with growth; the change points were taken to represent a change in growth due to an onset of maturity. The statistical model was given by

$$
\begin{array}{c}
y_{i} \sim N(\mu_{i}, \sigma^{2}) \\
\mu_i = \alpha + \theta X + \gamma_{1}(\mbox{length}_{i} - \omega_{t,s}) I (\mbox{length}_{i} \leq \omega_{t,s}) + \gamma_{2}(\mbox{length}_{i} - \omega_{t,s}) I (\mbox{length}_{i} \gt \omega_{t,s}) + \nu_{t} \\
\end{array}
$$
with

$$
\begin{array}{c}
\omega_{t,s} \sim N(\lambda_{t,s}, \sigma_{r})\\
\lambda_{t,s} = \zeta_{\mbox{sex}} \\

\nu_{t} \sim N(0, \sigma_{y})
\end{array}
$$

where $\y_{i}$ is the specific growth rate of individual $i$ calculated as the difference in length between recapture and capture divided by the days at liberty; $\mu_{i}$ is the expected specific growth rate for individual $i$; $\alpha$ is an average specific growth rate represented as an intercept; $\nu_{y}$ is a Gaussian random effect of year with variance $\sigma_{y}$ that allows the average specific growth rate to differ by years; $\theta$ = $\beta_{1}, \beta_{2}, ..., \beta_{M}$ is a vector of coefficients relating the influences of $M$ explanatory variables in matrix $X = X_{1}, X_{2}, ..., X_{M}$ on the expected specific growth rate; and the effect of individual length was included as a possibly year- ($t$) and sex- ($s$) specific change point $\omega_{t,s}$ where $\zeta_{\mbox{sex}}$ is the effect of sex on the change point; and $I(.)$ is an indicator function propagating the effect of length on specific growth rate depending on whether the length of individual $i$ of sex $s$ in year $t$ is less or equal to ($\gamma_{1}$) or greater than ($\gamma_{2}$) the estimated year- and sex-specific change point $\omega_{y,s}$.

The explanatory variables in $X$ were: *sex* included as a categorical variable with the first level (i.e. *s* = female) set to a contrast level, *trend* was included as a continuous effect of year, and *temperature* included as a continuous effect of degree days. 

<!-- Table 1 -->

```{r}
#| label: table1-inline
#| eval: !expr inline
#| echo: false
#| results: asis

## if table exists
if (file.exists(here("model-descriptions.xlsx"))) {

  ## read table
  tab1 <- read_xlsx(here("model-descriptions.xlsx"))
  
  ## convert to data.table
  tab1 <- data.table(tab1)
  
  ## optionally drop columns
  rm_cols <- c("Code")
  tab1 <- tab1[, -rm_cols, with = FALSE]
  
  ## print
  knitr::kable(tab1, caption = "**Table 1** Candidate model set fitted and compared to examine the empirical support for terms included in the models.")
  
} else {
  
  NULL

}

```




# Results (500)

Some exploratory plots:

<!-- Figure 1 -->

```{r}
#| label: figure1-inline
#| eval: !expr inline
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "**Figure 1** Temperature plot"

## if plot exists
if (file.exists(here("plots", "temperature-plot.jpg"))) {
  
  ## plot it
  knitr::include_graphics(here("plots", "temperature-plot.jpg"))
  
} else {
  
  NULL
  
}

```

<!-- Figure 2 -->

```{r}
#| label: figure2-inline
#| eval: !expr inline
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "**Figure 2** Tag year boxplot"

## if plot exists
if (file.exists(here("plots", "tagyear-growth-plot.jpg"))) {
  
  ## plot it
  knitr::include_graphics(here("plots", "tagyear-growth-plot.jpg"))
  
} else {
  
  NULL
  
}

```

<!-- Figure 3 -->

```{r}
#| label: figure3-inline
#| eval: !expr inline
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "**Figure 3** temperature growth plot"

## if plot exists
if (file.exists(here("plots", "temp-growth-plot.jpg"))) {
  
  ## plot it
  knitr::include_graphics(here("plots", "temp-growth-plot.jpg"))
  
} else {
  
  NULL
  
}

```

<!-- Figure 4 -->

```{r}
#| label: figure4-inline
#| eval: !expr inline
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "**Figure 4** growth sex plot"

## if plot exists
if (file.exists(here("plots", "growth-sex-plot.jpg"))) {
  
  ## plot it
  knitr::include_graphics(here("plots", "growth-sex-plot.jpg"))
  
} else {
  
  NULL
  
}

```

<!-- Figure 5 -->

```{r}
#| label: figure5-inline
#| eval: !expr inline
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "**Figure 5** temperature growth sex plot"

## if plot exists
if (file.exists(here("plots", "temp-growth-sex-plot.jpg"))) {
  
  ## plot it
  knitr::include_graphics(here("plots", "temp-growth-sex-plot.jpg"))
  
} else {
  
  NULL
  
}

```


# Discussion (800)

# Conclusion (200)

# Supplementary data

# Acknowledgements

# Conflict of interest statement

# Funding

# Data availability statement

# Author contributions statement

# References

::: {#refs}
:::

```{r}
#| eval: !expr endofms
#| result: asis
cat("\\newpage")
```

```{r}
#| eval: !expr endofms
#| results: asis
cat("# Tables \n")
```

<!-- Table 1 -->

```{r}
#| label: table1
#| eval: !expr endofms
#| echo: false
#| results: asis

## if table exists
if (file.exists(here("model-descriptions.xlsx"))) {

  ## read table
  tab1 <- read_xlsx(here("model-descriptions.xlsx"))
  
  ## convert to data.table
  tab1 <- data.table(tab1)
  
  ## optionally drop columns
  rm_cols <- c("Code")
  tab1 <- tab1[, -rm_cols, with = FALSE]
  
  ## print
  knitr::kable(tab1, caption = "**Table 1** Candidate model set fitted and compared to examine the empirical support for terms included in the models.")
  
} else {
  
  NULL

}

```

```{r}
#| eval: !expr endofms
#| result: asis
cat("\\newpage")
```

```{r}
#| eval: !expr endofms
#| results: asis
cat("# Figures \n")
```

<!-- Figure 1 -->

```{r}
#| label: figure1
#| eval: !expr endofms
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "**Figure 1** Temperature plot"

## if plot exists
if (file.exists(here("plots", "temperature-plot.jpg"))) {
  
  ## plot it
  knitr::include_graphics(here("plots", "temperature-plot.jpg"))
  
} else {
  
  NULL
  
}

```

<!-- Figure 2 -->

```{r}
#| label: figure2
#| eval: !expr endofms
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "**Figure 2** Tag year boxplot"

## if plot exists
if (file.exists(here("plots", "tagyear-growth-plot.jpg"))) {
  
  ## plot it
  knitr::include_graphics(here("plots", "tagyear-growth-plot.jpg"))
  
} else {
  
  NULL
  
}

```

<!-- Figure 3 -->

```{r}
#| label: figure3
#| eval: !expr endofms
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "**Figure 3** temperature growth plot"

## if plot exists
if (file.exists(here("plots", "temp-growth-plot.jpg"))) {
  
  ## plot it
  knitr::include_graphics(here("plots", "temp-growth-plot.jpg"))
  
} else {
  
  NULL
  
}

```

<!-- Figure 4 -->

```{r}
#| label: figure4
#| eval: !expr endofms
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "**Figure 4** growth sex plot"

## if plot exists
if (file.exists(here("plots", "growth-sex-plot.jpg"))) {
  
  ## plot it
  knitr::include_graphics(here("plots", "growth-sex-plot.jpg"))
  
} else {
  
  NULL
  
}

```

<!-- Figure 5 -->

```{r}
#| label: figure5
#| eval: !expr endofms
#| echo: false
#| fig-cap: "**Figure 5** temperature growth sex plot"

## if plot exists
if (file.exists(here("plots", "temp-growth-sex-plot.jpg"))) {
  
  ## plot it
  knitr::include_graphics(here("plots", "temp-growth-sex-plot.jpg"))
  
} else {
  
  NULL
  
}

```
